candidates.pr_title: test(checker): cover mapped key TS2322 anchors
candidates.body: `diagnostic_anchor_text` slices `source` using unchecked byte indices derived from `diagnostic.start`/`length`. If a diagnostic ever points into a different file (e.g. lib) or reports a span outside `source`, this will panic with an unhelpful “byte index out of bounds”/“not a char boundary” message. Consider using `source.get(start..end).expect(...)` (and optionally asserting `diagnostic.file == "test.ts"`) so failures produce a clear message and avoid panics on invalid spans.

candidates.pr_title: docs(solver): clarify interner perf-counter cost summary
candidates.body: The wording "the optimizer folds into the no-op path" overstates what the compiler can guarantee here. Since `pc` depends on a runtime `enabled_fast()` result, the `if let Some(c) = pc` checks still compile to an Option discriminant test + branch; they’re just predictable/cheap when `pc` is `None`. Consider rephrasing to describe the actual runtime shape (predictable branch, body skipped), without implying it is optimized away.

candidates.pr_title: test(checker): cover max constraints diagnostic display
candidates.body: This test picks the first diagnostic with code 2345 via `find`, which doesn’t guarantee it’s the diagnostic for `max2(1, 2)` if additional TS2345s ever appear (ordering can change as the checker evolves). To make the test unambiguous, filter TS2345 diagnostics and select the one whose `start` matches `first_arg_start` (or assert there’s exactly one TS2345 before using it).

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): preserve higher-order generic call inference
candidates.body: `collect_all_types` returns an `FxHashSet`, so iterating it here makes the order of `hoisted` (and therefore the synthesized function `type_params`) depend on hash iteration order. Since `type_params` ordering is part of the `FunctionShape` identity and affects diagnostics, this can introduce non-deterministic behavior/flaky snapshots. Consider collecting the referenced `__infer_src_*` params into a Vec and sorting deterministically (e.g., by `Atom`/name) before assigning to `shape.type_params`.

candidates.pr_title: docs(checker): clarify cross-file-query helper module docstring
candidates.body: The module-level docstring implies that the helpers in this module collapse `TypeId::UNKNOWN` to `None`, but that isn’t true for all helpers here (e.g. `cached_cross_file_class_instance_type` explicitly does *not* filter `ERROR`/`UNKNOWN`/`ANY`; see the note on that function). Consider tightening this wording to refer specifically to the `cached_cross_file_symbol_type`/`SymbolType` path (or say “some/most helpers”) so the module docs don’t conflict with the behavior documented below.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): widen overflow tuple boolean display
candidates.body:
`target_element` is looked up only via `elements.get(element_position)`, so for tuple targets with a rest element (variadic tuples) any element past `elements.len()` is treated as “overflow” with no contextual slot. That loses the fact those positions are still contextually typed by the tuple’s rest element, which can change the intended literal-preservation / display behavior.

Consider resolving `target_element` for out-of-range positions via the tuple’s rest element (e.g. `elements.iter().rev().find(|e| e.rest)`) so rest-backed positions are treated as target-backed too.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): contextually type empty arrays in logical OR
candidates.body: This new regression test relies on standard-library utility types (`Omit`, `Partial`), but `check_source_diagnostics` runs with lib definitions disabled (see `test_utils.rs`), so the snippet will produce TS2304/TS2552-style “cannot find name” errors and the test can still pass because it only asserts on TS2345. Define minimal `Partial`/`Omit` (and any dependencies like `Pick`/`Exclude`) in the snippet, or broaden the assertion to fail on unexpected diagnostics so the test actually validates the intended behavior.

candidates.pr_title: fix(binder): guard persistent scope count overflow with better error message
candidates.body: This still panics on overflow via `expect(...)` (only the message changed). Since the PR description/linked issue describes the crash as the bug to fix (and suggests returning/propagating an internal error or hard-capping), consider making this path non-panicking (e.g., change this API to return `Result` and bubble an internal diagnostic) or update the PR/issue linkage to reflect that this is only an improved panic message, not a behavioral fix.

candidates.pr_title: fix(parser): guard NodeArena::len_u32 overflow with better error message
candidates.body: There are existing tests elsewhere that assert overflow panic messages contain a specific constant (e.g., `SOURCE_FILE_LEN_OVERFLOW_MESSAGE` in `crates/tsz-core/src/source_file/mod.rs`). Since this PR changes the NodeArena overflow message, it would be good to add a similar regression test in this file’s `#[cfg(test)]` module to lock in the new wording and avoid accidental message regressions.

candidates.pr_title: Fix generic union in-operand diagnostics
candidates.body: The TS2322 assertion is order-dependent on the printed union type (`"T | { a: string; }"`). Union member ordering appears to be non-deterministic elsewhere in the test suite (some tests accept either `A | B` or `B | A`), so this can make the test flaky. Consider matching both orderings or asserting on smaller substrings (e.g., that the message contains both `"T"` and `"{ a: string; }"`) instead of the full union rendering.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): widen overflow tuple boolean display
candidates.body: `target_element` is computed via `elements.get(element_position)`, which only works for fixed tuple slots. For target tuples with a `...rest` element, positions beyond `elements.len()` are still contextually typed by the rest element, but this code will treat them as overflow and force the widened fallback (dropping `true`/`false` preservation and skipping `literal_expression_display`). Consider resolving the contextual element with a rest fallback (e.g., when `element_position >= len`, use the last `rest` element if present) so rest-typed positions behave like other contextually typed tuple slots, while fixed-length overflow still widens.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): preserve enum member union diagnostics
candidates.body: `format_union_with_collapsed_enum_display` now pushes formatted enum member names into `rendered` but still returns `None` unless `collapsed_enum` is set. For unions composed entirely of enum members (the case this change targets), this causes the code to fall back to the diagnostic `TypeFormatter`, which (per `tsz-solver/src/diagnostics/format/compound.rs:909+`) collapses same-enum member unions back to the parent enum name, so the member names won’t actually be preserved. Consider returning `Some(rendered.join(" | "))` when you’ve rendered any enum member names (or otherwise short-circuiting before the formatter runs) so the preservation behavior takes effect.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): align recursiveFunctionTypes display fingerprints
candidates.body: `should_elide_recursive_typeof_function_return` currently returns true for any nested Function/Callable return type that contains a `TypeQuery` anywhere in its signature, regardless of whether it’s actually part of a self-referential recursion. That means diagnostic-mode formatting can collapse non-recursive higher-order returns to `...` as well. If the intent is to match tsc’s recursive-cycle elision only, consider tightening the predicate (or adding a regression test proving non-recursive `typeof` nested returns should/shouldn’t elide).

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): report JS @type subclass assignment mismatch
candidates.body: The raw initializer re-check in the `jsdoc_new_expression_relation` branch clears the initializer cache and then calls `get_type_of_node_with_request(..., TypingRequest::NONE)`, which will repopulate `ctx.node_types` for the initializer with the non-contextual (raw) type. That overwrites the earlier contextually-typed initializer entry you intentionally seeded into `node_types` (to prevent later context-free lookups from widening/losing inference), and can change downstream behavior/fingerprints. Consider preserving/restoring the contextual cached initializer type after the raw check (e.g., save the current cached type and re-insert `init_type`), or compute the raw type under a snapshot/rollback that also restores `node_types`/request caches rather than only diagnostics.

candidates.pr_title: fix(dts): emit js function type tag signatures
candidates.body: Parameter splitting in `parse_jsdoc_function_type_signature` uses `params_text.split(',')`, which will split inside nested types that contain commas (tuples, generics, function types, object types, etc.) and can produce incorrect `name/type` pairs that still pass validation, leading to invalid emitted signatures. Consider reusing the existing `split_jsdoc_params` + `parse_function_type_param_decl`/`find_top_level_colon` helpers (already used elsewhere in this file) to split only on top-level commas and locate the top-level colon safely.

candidates.pr_title: chore(emitter,binder-tests): share parse_test_source helper across src-mounted test modules
candidates.body: `parse_test_source` takes `&str` and then immediately allocates via `source.to_string()`. In tests that already build a `String` (e.g. the joined `source` used later in this module), this introduces an extra clone/allocation versus moving the existing `String`. Consider adding a variant that takes `String`, or making the helper generic over `Into<String>` and adjusting call sites that don't need to retain the original string.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): expand index-signature alias receiver display
candidates.body: `diagnostic_messages` calls `load_default_lib_files()` on every invocation, and this helper is used by multiple tests in this file. Since `load_default_lib_files()` reads and parses lib assets from disk, consider caching the loaded libs (e.g., a `OnceLock<Vec<Arc<LibFile>>>`) so the test suite doesn’t repeatedly reload the same lib bundle.

candidates.pr_title: fix(cli): anchor TS2578 at comment range, not at line start
candidates.body: `find_directive_in_text` still returns a `(DirectiveKind, usize)` but the `usize` (relative offset) is no longer used now that `directive_text_start` was removed. Consider changing the helper to return only `DirectiveKind` (or otherwise using the offset) to avoid carrying an unused value through the API and to make the intent clearer.

candidates.pr_title: fix(cli): anchor TS2578 at comment range, not at line start
candidates.body: The PR description claims “new failures: 0” and a category delta of `false_positive 0`, but the updated conformance snapshot shows `false_positive: 1` with `TS2589` listed under extra/false-positive codes. Please either update the PR description to reflect the new TS2589 false positive (and any related baseline/detail changes) or regenerate the conformance artifacts if this is unintended/stale.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): preserve literal constraint display for generic calls
candidates.body: The candidate deduplication uses `candidates.contains(&candidate)` inside the loop, which makes this O(n^2) in the number of literal candidates. Consider tracking seen candidates with a small `FxHashSet`/`IndexSet` (or similar) while building `candidates` to keep this linear and simpler to reason about as argument counts grow (e.g., large rest-arg calls).

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): defer parameter-dependent recursive alias TS2589
candidates.body: `conditional_body_has_unresolved_computed_recursive_alias_ref(...)` is called unconditionally here, even when `body_is_conditional` is false. Since the TS2589 definition-site check is intended to apply only to conditional aliases, consider gating this computation behind `body_is_conditional` (or otherwise ensuring the unresolved-computed path can’t trigger for non-conditional aliases).

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): preserve declared alias application display
candidates.body: `recursive_tuple_declared_assignment_types` special-cases by `def.name == "TupleOf"` but doesn't verify the definition kind. If a user defines an `interface TupleOf<...>` (or other non-alias DefKind) this branch can incorrectly short-circuit assignability and emit TS2322 unconditionally. Consider also requiring `def.kind == DefKind::TypeAlias` (similar to `declared_same_alias_application_assignment_types`) before applying this shortcut.

candidates.pr_title: test: cover nested object playground shape
candidates.body: This assertion only checks that TS2322 is absent; the test would still pass if other semantic diagnostics are emitted (meaning the snippet is not actually diagnostic-free). Consider asserting that the semantic diagnostics array is empty (and optionally that syntactic diagnostics are empty) to match the “accepts … assignment” expectation.

candidates.pr_title: docs(solver): clarify interner perf-counter cost summary
candidates.body: This repeats the phrase "optimizer folds into the no-op path", which can be read as a compile-time guarantee. Because `pc` is determined from a runtime `enabled_fast()` check, the generated code still includes an Option test/branch even when disabled; it’s just predictable and cheap. Recommend mirroring the more precise phrasing used earlier (e.g., "predictable branch" / "body skipped") rather than claiming it folds away.

candidates.pr_title: fix(solver): preserve normalized object literal display
candidates.body: `normalized_display_properties` does a linear `.find()` over `original_display` for every normalized property, making this O(n²) per object. Consider precomputing a name->PropertyInfo map (or index) from `original_display` once and doing O(1) lookups while mapping `normalized_properties`.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): preserve strict callback assignability diagnostics
candidates.body: `callable_type_after_display_evaluation` can trigger multiple evaluation passes (`evaluate_type_with_resolution`, `evaluate_type_for_assignability`, etc.). In this hunk it’s invoked unconditionally, even when `depth != 0` (where `strict_callback_case` can never be true). Consider guarding these calls behind `if depth == 0 { ... }` (or computing them lazily only when needed) to avoid extra work during nested failure rendering.

candidates.pr_title: docs(perf): plan §1 — refresh "Diagnostics timing" and "Perf counters" baseline rows
candidates.body: This row is listing `WiredCounters` field names, but a couple are abbreviated in a way that doesn’t match the actual struct fields (`interner_per_kind` and `resolver_fs_probes` in `crates/tsz-common/src/perf_counters.rs`). Suggest using the exact flag names (and optionally stating explicitly that all are `true` *except* `interner_lock_wait`, which depends on `perf-counters-timing`) to avoid future confusion/search misses.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): skip contravariant slots in same-base constrained-target rejection
candidates.body: Variance lookup here bypasses the QueryDatabase variance cache that `check_application_variance_assignability` just consulted/populated (it only checks declared variances, then recomputes). Consider looking up `self.ctx.types.get_type_param_variance(def_id)` first and inserting the computed result so this heuristic doesn’t repeatedly recompute variances on hot assignability paths.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): report generic rest satisfies TS2345 at argument
candidates.body: The `#[cfg(test)]` attribute is duplicated around this module inclusion. It’s harmless, but redundant and can be collapsed to a single `#[cfg(test)]` for the `generic_rest_satisfies_anchor_tests` module block to keep the test-module list consistent and easier to scan.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): report class-expression field this misses
candidates.body: `get_type_of_node_with_request` already performs class-expression member checking in `dispatch.rs` (and intentionally skips it in re-entrant cases via `class_constructor_resolution_set`). Calling `check_direct_class_expression_initializer` unconditionally here can (a) double-check the same class expression and duplicate diagnostics, and (b) bypass the re-entrancy guard that avoids false TS2339s. Consider guarding this call to only run when the dispatch path would have skipped member checking (re-entrant), or refactor the dispatch/class-expression typing path so the needed "this miss" diagnostics are reported without a second pass.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): report class-expression field this misses
candidates.body: This new cached-instance-`this` guard avoids returning `ANY`/`ERROR` for the general instance-member path, but the earlier explicit `this: CurrentClass` fast path still returns `cached_instance_this_type` without filtering. Since `cached_instance_this_type` can be set to `TypeId::ANY` (e.g., cycle-breaking in `get_class_instance_type`), the explicit-`this` case can still incorrectly return `ANY` and hide downstream diagnostics. Apply the same `ANY`/`ERROR` filtering (or `type_contains_error`) to the explicit-`this` cached return as well.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): preserve numeric union aliases in TS2322
candidates.body: `declared_numeric_literal_union_alias_source_display` lookup chain is repeated here (and again later when computing `source_from_annotation`). Consider computing the declared-display once and reusing it to avoid duplicated AST queries/type evaluation during diagnostic rendering and reduce the chance of the two call sites drifting in behavior.

candidates.pr_title: fix(cli): allow emit after removed import flags
candidates.body: `is_removed_option_diagnostic_code` currently returns true for both TS5102 (removed option key) and TS5108 (removed option value like `target=ES3`). The function name suggests only "removed option" keys; consider renaming to reflect that it also covers removed *values* (or split into two helpers) to avoid future misuse.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): align objectRestNegative rest assignment fingerprint
candidates.body: This replaces the centralized `check_assignable_or_report_at_exact_anchor` pipeline with a manual `is_assignable_to` + one-line TS2322. That bypasses the normal “explain” path and can drop related-information elaboration (e.g. “Types of property 'a' are incompatible.”), as well as other assignability-special cases handled by the reporter (weak-type TS2559 substitution, exactOptionalPropertyTypes variants, etc.). Consider emitting via the standard failure rendering while still forcing the top-level display types to come from the computed `rest_type`/`rest_target_type` (e.g. render the solver failure reason with explicit display types) so we keep both the correct fingerprint and the richer diagnostic details.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): align recursiveFunctionTypes display fingerprints
candidates.body: `direct_param_display` is computed before `overloaded_recursive_typeof_parameter_display`, but in the early-return path it’s unused. Since `format_type_diagnostic` can be relatively expensive, consider moving the `direct_param_display` computation below this new fast-path (or compute it lazily only when needed).

candidates.pr_title: fix(dts): expand returned rest tuple aliases
candidates.body: Unlabeled tuple elements (e.g. `[string, number]`) are valid TS, but expansion currently requires `name: type` labels via `split_once(':')`, so it will fail and fall back to the unexpanded rest param. Consider synthesizing stable parameter names for unlabeled tuple elements so this expansion works for common tuple forms.

candidates.pr_title: fix(dts): expand returned rest tuple aliases
candidates.body: `unique_parameter_name` only de-dupes within the expanded tuple elements. If earlier parameters in the same signature already use a tuple-element label (e.g. an existing `x` param and a tuple element also labeled `x`), expansion can emit duplicate parameter names. Seed the `seen` list with previously-emitted parameter names when expanding so names are unique across the whole parameter list.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): accept React component type unions
candidates.body: The union-member early-return isn’t gated by `!is_this_tag`, so `<this />` could be incorrectly treated as valid when its `component_type` is a union whose members allow props extraction. This contradicts the earlier “Keep `this` tags strict” behavior—consider adding the same `!is_this_tag` guard around this union shortcut.

candidates.pr_title: fix(emitter): report symlinked import-ref TS2883
candidates.body: This test only cleans up the temp directory at the very end. If any `expect!`/`assert_eq!` above fails, the temp tree is left behind under the system temp dir. Consider using a guard (e.g., `tempfile::TempDir` or a `Drop` cleanup guard) so cleanup runs even when the test fails/panics.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): emit TS7008 for JS null constructor members
candidates.body: The verification command includes an absolute local path (`/Users/...`) which is machine-specific and embeds a username. Prefer a repo-relative path, a variable (e.g. `$TS_TESTS_DIR`), or a short note describing the invocation without hardcoding a local filesystem path.

candidates.pr_title: fix(dts): emit js function type tag signatures
candidates.body: `parse_jsdoc_function_type_signature` finds the parameter list close-paren via `after_params.find(')')`, which stops at the first `)` and will mis-parse valid function types when a parameter type contains nested parentheses (e.g. callbacks like `(cb: (x: T) => void) => void`). This can lead to emitting an invalid `.d.ts` signature rather than safely falling back. Use the existing `find_matching_paren` (already in this module) or another balanced-paren scan to locate the matching `)` for the top-level parameter list before extracting `params_text`/`return_type`.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): stabilize recursive Box2 fingerprint
candidates.body: This rewrite matches via `contains(...)` but then overwrites the entire `message_text`. If the TS2322 message ever includes additional elaboration lines (e.g. "Type ...\n  Property ..." patterns used elsewhere), this would silently drop that extra detail. Consider making the match stricter (e.g. equality/starts_with) and/or doing a targeted replacement of just the target display (replace "Box<number | Box2>" → "Box2") so any trailing detail is preserved.

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): align recursiveTypeReferences1 array diagnostics
candidates.body: The diagnostic suppression here is based only on exact `message_text` matches, so it will remove *any* TS2322 in this file with one of these messages, even if it comes from an unrelated assignment. To keep this rewrite from masking legitimate diagnostics, consider additionally narrowing the filter by span (e.g., only suppress when `diag.start` is within/near the known `flat/flat1/flat2` call sites, similar to the nearby-source check used in other rewrite helpers in this file).

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): align recursiveTypeReferences1 array diagnostics
candidates.body: This rewrite unconditionally suppresses the listed TS2322 messages once the top-level `source_text.contains(...)` guard passes, but it only re-adds the expected diagnostics if the specific `flat([1, ['a']]);` / `flat1...` / `flat2...` markers are found. If a source file happens to match the guard strings but not these call-site markers (or if the upstream conformance snippet changes), this will drop diagnostics without restoring them. Consider extending the initial guard to include the call markers, or only performing the suppression after confirming all three anchors exist.

candidates.pr_title: fix(dts): ignore cjs exports in esm js modules
candidates.body: `source_file_has_native_esm_syntax` walks every top-level statement; it’s called multiple times during per-file setup (e.g., several `collect_js_*` helpers), which adds repeated O(n) passes over the same statement list. Consider computing this once per source file (store a boolean on `DeclarationEmitter` during setup and reuse it) to avoid extra scans on large JS files.

candidates.pr_title: fix(dts): ignore cjs exports in esm js modules
candidates.body: `source_file_has_native_esm_syntax` treats any `EXPORT_ASSIGNMENT` as “native ESM”, but in this codebase JS files can contain `export =` (see existing `test_js_export_equals_emits_before_target_declaration`). That means a JS file using `export =` will be misclassified as “native ESM” and will now skip CommonJS export synthesis/analysis guarded by this helper. Consider checking `EXPORT_ASSIGNMENT` nodes and only treating them as native ESM when they are *not* `export =` (i.e., `export default`), so `export =` JS files keep their CommonJS behavior.

candidates.pr_title: fix(dts): emit js function keyword property aliases
candidates.body: `reserved_names` is documented as tracking names taken in the *top-level scope* of the file. Inserting namespace member locals into this set (including when no alias is needed) can force unrelated later top-level imports/aliases to be renamed even though namespace members are scoped. Consider keeping a separate JS late-bound namespace-local name set (or only reserving within `reserved_member_names`) instead of mutating `self.reserved_names` here.

candidates.pr_title: fix(dts): emit js function keyword property aliases
candidates.body: When renaming a `namespace_member_name` due to a global collision, `generate_unique_name(namespace_member_name)` only checks `self.reserved_names`, not the already-known `reserved_member_names` for the current namespace. This can pick a name that exists as another member in the same namespace (e.g., choosing `normal_1` when there is already a `normal_1` property), producing duplicate declarations and an invalid `.d.ts`. Ensure the chosen synthetic name is unique against the current namespace’s member names as well (e.g., seed `reserved_names` with `reserved_member_names` before generating, or generate against both sets).

candidates.pr_title: fix(checker): map variadic tuple source display through rest slots
candidates.body: `target_tuple_element_for_literal_position` linearly scans `elements` to find the rest index on every literal element (`iter().position(...)`), which makes building a diagnostic O(literal_len * target_len) for large tuples. Consider computing `rest_index`/`suffix_len` once in `array_literal_tuple_source_type_display` (or caching it) and passing the precomputed mapping info into the per-element helper.
